message:
fix(net,grpc,io): cap waker vecs at 32 entries, defend WASM RefCell borrows, fix DNS test min_ttl

Three defensive improvements across networking and I/O:

1. Bounded waker vectors (listener.rs, virtual_tcp.rs, unix/listener.rs,
   grpc/client.rs):

   All AcceptWaiters and ResponseStreamState waker registrations now cap
   at 32 entries, evicting the oldest waker (remove(0)) when full. This
   prevents unbounded memory growth from misbehaving or rapidly-spawning
   callers without requiring the complexity of ID-tracked wakers.

   The TCP listener also reverts the AcceptWaiter/AcceptFuture refactor
   from the prior commit series, returning to the simpler Vec<Waker>
   approach with will_wake() deduplication. The 32-entry cap provides
   the safety guarantee that the refactor was trying to achieve, with
   less surface area.

2. WASM RefCell borrow defense (browser_stream.rs):

   MessagePort and BroadcastChannel error callbacks now use
   try_borrow_mut() instead of borrow_mut() on the shared inbox Rc<RefCell>.
   In re-entrant browser event scenarios, borrow_mut() would panic;
   try_borrow_mut() logs the collision and drops the incoming error
   message instead, avoiding a WASM abort.

3. DNS resolver test fix (resolver.rs):

   A negative-TTL caching test now explicitly sets `min_ttl: Duration::ZERO`
   in its CacheConfig, preventing the test from accidentally depending on
   whatever the default min_ttl happens to be.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
